steps.title: Find the central finding
steps.content: Every sentence centers on the discovery that Saturn now has more than 100 observed moons. The telescope and Jupiter comparison both support the point about Saturn's moon count.

steps.title: Avoid side details
steps.content: B focuses on how the telescope works, which the text never explains. C invents a focus on Jupiter's atmosphere. D treats Hawaii as the topic even though it is only the telescope's location. The purpose is the new Saturn finding. The answer is A.
